name: Swimm API
what_it_does: Code documentation platform with REST API that auto-syncs documentation with code changes, helping developers keep docs up-to-date and onboard faster with context-aware explanations.
use_cases: Automatically flagging stale documentation when related code files are modified via webhooks
Querying existing documentation coverage for specific files or directories
Creating or updating documentation stubs programmatically during code generation
Tracking onboarding progress and documentation gaps across repositories
Integrating code documentation validation into pull request review workflows
not_for: General team wiki or knowledge management (use Notion/Confluence instead)
Projects with minimal team onboarding needs
best_when: An agent generates code that needs accompanying documentation, or when maintaining documentation for large enterprise codebases with frequent changes.
avoid_when: Your team is small (<5 developers) or your codebase is primarily maintained by a single developer.

auth.notes: OAuth2 for GitHub/GitLab integration. API keys for programmatic access. Workspace-level authentication.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free for up to 5 users and 1 repository
pricing.notes: Free tier suitable for small teams to evaluate. VS Code extension available.

performance.rate_limits: Standard API rate limits; not publicly documented

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API documentation is not comprehensive. Most integration is through the VS Code extension and GitHub app rather than raw API. Limited agent ecosystem.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Document creation should check for existing docs first. GitHub app integration handles staleness detection automatically.
known_agent_gotchas: Primary interaction is through editor extension or GitHub app, not REST API
Documentation stale detection relies on GitHub webhooks being properly configured
API documentation is not publicly available — requires account to access
Small community — limited third-party tooling and client libraries

security_score_components.security_notes: OAuth2 for GitHub/GitLab integration follows standard patterns. SOC2 Type II certified. Code is accessed read-only for documentation purposes.
